name: La Serra de Bèrnia — Circular por La Solana y el Barranco del Maseroff
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Benissa traverse a diverse landscape, combining rugged mountain ranges with a dramatic coastline. The region features prominent mountains like the Sierra de Bèrnia and Sierra de Oltà, offering challenging ascents and panoramic views. Inland, agricultural valleys such as the Vall de Pop provide routes through vineyards and traditional farmlands. This varied terrain ensures a range of mountain biking experiences, from coastal paths to technical mountain trails. Best mountain bike trails around Benissa The most popular mountain bike trail is La Serra de Bèrnia — Circular por La Solana y el Barranco del Maseroff, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the technical demands and scenic views of the Sierra de Bèrnia mountain range. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Saladar Saltworks – Salinas de Calp Viewpoint loop from Benissa, a moderate 19.4 miles (31.2 km) path. This route offers coastal perspectives and views of the saltworks near Calp. Local mountain bikers also love the Molí de Lliber – Top Singletrail loop from Benissa, a 26.8 miles (43.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Benissa is defined by mountainous interiors, coastal paths, and agricultural valleys. The network offers options for easy rides, moderate challenges, and difficult ascents, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Benissa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Benissa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Benissa?
A: Benissa offers a diverse network of over 30 mountain bike trails. These routes traverse varied landscapes, from rugged mountain ranges to agricultural valleys and coastal paths, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Benissa?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Benissa suitable for mountain biking almost year-round. Spring (February-May) is particularly beautiful, with almond trees in bloom, offering scenic rides. Autumn (September-November) also provides pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails in Benissa suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Benissa has several easy mountain bike trails. For a gentle ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40393790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Moraira – Moraira Beaches and Promenade loop from Pepe la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.8 km long and offers coastal views. The Muscat Route, mentioned in regional research, also provides an easier itinerary through agricultural paths and vineyards.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Benissa?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the Sierra de Bèrnia and Sierra de Oltà mountain ranges offer technical and demanding routes. A prime example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924836235/la-serra-de-bernia-circular-por-la-solana-y-el-barranco-del-maseroff" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serra de Bèrnia — Circular por La Solana y el Barranco del Maseroff&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 37.3 km trail with significant elevation changes and technical sections.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails in Benissa?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Benissa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saladar Saltworks – Salinas de Calp Viewpoint loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.2 km circular route offering coastal perspectives. The Sierra de Bèrnia also features a well-known circular route, PR-CV 7, which provides panoramic views.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on mountain bike trails around Benissa?
A: Mountain bike trails in Benissa offer a stunning variety of views. You can expect breathtaking panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean Sea, including the iconic Peñón de Ifach, from mountain ranges like Sierra de Bèrnia and Sierra de Oltà. Inland routes provide picturesque scenes of vineyards, almond groves, and traditional terraced fields in areas like the Vall de Pop.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Benissa?
A: The mountain bike trails around Benissa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from nearly 50 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic coastal paths, and the stunning views of both the sea and the mountainous interior.

Q: Are there any interesting natural attractions or landmarks along the mountain bike routes?
A: Absolutely! Many routes pass by or near notable attractions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saladar Saltworks – Salinas de Calp Viewpoint loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saladar Saltworks&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7344015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Calp Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, where you might even spot &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8054904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;flamingos&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Bèrnia also features the historic 16th-century 'El Fort de Bernia'.

Q: Can I explore mountain passes in the Benissa area by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Benissa includes several notable passes that can be incorporated into mountain bike routes. Highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1851315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bèrnia Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/509914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lemon Pass&lt;/a&gt; offer challenging climbs and rewarding descents with spectacular views.

Q: Are there any routes that combine mountain biking with coastal scenery?
A: Benissa's unique location allows for routes that beautifully blend mountain biking with coastal scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saladar Saltworks – Salinas de Calp Viewpoint loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering coastal perspectives and views of the saltworks. The region's 4 km coastline, with coves like Cala Baladrar and Cala Fustera, also provides opportunities for scenic rides along the clifftops, such as the Paseo Ecológico.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through agricultural areas or vineyards?
A: Yes, many routes in Benissa lead through the region's rich agricultural landscapes. The Vall de Pop, for instance, is characterized by vineyards and almond trees. The Muscat Route is an easier itinerary that specifically connects Benissa with the Vall de Pop via agricultural paths, allowing you to explore vineyards and towns known for Muscat grape production.
tips: Very entertaining path with very nice views, both for walking and for mtb. By sectors there is loose stone but in general it can be circulated without problems and it is very entertaining.

Beautiful route on gravel, with no further difficulty. The effort of the journey is rewarded with a great view. The Mirador Tossal del Navarro is a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts as it offers stunning panoramic views and a connection to the rich history and geography of the region. The tour is worthwhile for anyone looking for peace, nature and adventure.
Sites with incredible views towards the coast of La Marina Alta. It is located a few meters from the road or can be accessed by the Senda de Bernia

Site with amazing views towards the Serra de Bèrnia and its impressive walls. It is reached by paved road and there is parking for cars. From here we start the ascent along the path towards Fort de Bèrnia. A few meters away is a restaurant with beautiful views.

Very picturesque and entertaining path that allows you to reach the Fort de Bernia. It is a trail designed for hiking but it can be ridden on a mtb although it has some technical sections with a lot of loose stones and the slope to overcome is high so it can be done without rushing... the views are totally amazing once you get there to the ruins of the fort.
The most picturesque and entertaining section is the one that runs through the Morro del Penyal next to the cliff with impressive views.
Totally recommended for both mtb and hiking!

The Fort of Bèrnia, built in 1562 by the royal engineer Juan Bautista Antoneli, was a clear and magnificent exponent of the Renaissance military architecture of the Ottomans on the coast. In 1612, and given its ineffectiveness due to its distance from the coast, King Philip III ordered its demolition.

Of the Fort you can still see the arches, the moat, the bastion, the cistern and the remains of the walls. Its extension was 10 meters long by 110 meters wide. It is declared B.I.C. (Asset of Cultural Interest) since 1997.

The explanatory panels show the former functioning and use of the Fort.

Ravine with beautiful views towards the Serra de Ferrer in a very nice environment. There is the Masserof path that runs through the ravine, in sectors it is quite neglected and if you travel by mtb you will have to get off and push the bike in several sections.
Very entertaining path between trees and in good condition with very nice views of the Xalo Valley.

waypoints.name: View of the Sierra de Bernia and the coast
